title: OVERDRIVE merch-brand logo finishing: variant set, transparent PNGs, SVG vector source, palette + font kit, usage board
one_line_ask: Take the client's supplied OVERDRIVE raster logo lockup and productionize it into the full merch brand-kit deliverable set — transparent primary/stacked/icon/avatar PNGs, social-PFP crop, black/white/mono variants, a clean SVG vector source, an extracted palette reference, font recommendations, and a usage moodboard — chaining every step in Adobe.

issues: Graduation cap sits slightly above and overlapping the 'O' rather than cleanly beside or fully integrated — minor compositional awkwardness a designer would need to adjust
Tassel rendered in gold which is correct but is very thin and could lose detail at small reproduction sizes

Slight drop-shadow/offset effect on the wordmark letters (navy text with gold outline/shadow) gives a very subtle pseudo-3D feel — acceptable as a source file but designer should flatten to true 2-color on vectorization
Background is off-white/cream rather than pure neutral gray — within acceptable source-file tolerance
Very slight luminance noise visible in shadow areas — acceptable for source file
Drawstring tips (aglets) catch highlight and could distract if not masked, but within brief tolerance
Fold crease on lower-left is moderately deep — may need minor dodge in post, but within brief scope
